commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new `tokenomics` plugin with a model-usage spend ledger, Gateway diagnostics report route, generated plugin docs, package metadata, tests, and a lockfile importer entry.

PR surface: Source +1017, Tests +168, Docs +81, Config +50, Other +6. Total +1322 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible for the blocking PR behavior: current main grants `internalDiagnostics` only to `diagnostics-otel` and `diagnostics-prometheus`, so this `tokenomics` service would take its unavailable-capability path. No live Gateway run was executed.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Automation should not repair this yet; maintainers first need to decide whether tokenomics belongs as an official bundled diagnostics plugin, an external ClawHub plugin, or a new generic diagnostics-consumer SDK surface.

target.title: feat: add tokenomics plugin for local-first LLM spend accounting
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Operators running OpenClaw have no built-in way to see what their model usage actually costs. Token usage is emitted as diagnostics, but there is no durable record of per-call spend and no report that answers "how much did we spend, on which models, and how much did free/local models save us?" Today that requires bolting on external tooling or scraping logs.

This adds a `tokenomics` plugin: a local-first LLM spend ledger plus a spend report, surfaced on the Gateway.

## Why This Change Was Made

The plugin registers a startup service that subscribes to internal `model.usage` diagnostics and appends one row per model call to an append-only JSONL ledger under the plugin state dir. It then serves a read-only spend report on `GET /api/diagnostics/tokenomics` (trusted-operator scope): period and by-model rollups, a free-vs-paid split, and an avoided-spend / counterfactual headline priced against a frontier baseline.

Key design decisions and boundaries:
- **Cost precedence:** the host-reported `costUsd` is authoritative (it is the truth for what was billed). When a call has no cost, a free classification yields `$0`; otherwise an optional local pricing catalog (`pricing.json`) estimates it. Unknown models stay `$0` so cost is never invented.
- **Offline and host-neutral:** nothing leaves the machine and no pricing feed is fetched at runtime. The ingestion seam is a plain `UsageEvent`, so the same core is reusable outside OpenClaw.
- **Non-goals:** no background network calls, no automatic price-list fetching, no UI beyond the diagnostics route. The pricing catalog is operator-supplied.

Implemented as an installable extension (per CONTRIBUTING guidance that features land as plugins on the plugin SDK), following the `diagnostics-prometheus` extension as the structural template.

## User Impact

Operators can now:
- Get a durable, append-only record of model spend with zero external dependencies.
- Query a spend report on the Gateway:
  - `GET /api/diagnostics/tokenomics` (JSON, last 30 days)
  - `?format=text` for a rendered terminal table
  - `?since=2026-06-01&until=2026-06-30&gran=day` for an explicit window
- See free-vs-paid breakdowns and an avoided-spend estimate that quantifies what local/free models saved versus a frontier baseline.

Install: `openclaw plugins install @openclaw/tokenomics`, then `openclaw plugins enable tokenomics`.

## Evidence

Validated locally on this branch:

- Extension test lane: `pnpm test:extension tokenomics`
  ```
  [test-extension] Running 1 test files for tokenomics
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  8 passed (8)
  ```
  Tests cover cost precedence (host cost wins), pricing fallback + unknown-model $0, ledger append/read, and the free-vs-paid report (avoided/counterfactual math).
- Typecheck (canonical extension test project): `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — pass.
- Lint: `oxlint extensions/tokenomics` — clean.
- Format: `oxfmt --check extensions/tokenomics` — clean.
- Docs: regenerated via `pnpm plugins:inventory:gen`; `pnpm plugins:inventory:check` — pass (plugin inventory + reference pages updated).

Lockfile change is the single workspace importer entry for the new package.
